name: Spear of Destiny
description: The Spear of Destiny, also known as the Holy Lance, is a legendary weapon believed to be the spear that pierced the side of Jesus Christ during his crucifixion. According to Christian tradition, the spear possesses immense power, granting invincibility and the ability to control destiny to whoever wields it. Ownership of the spear has been attributed to various historical figures, including Roman emperors, Holy Roman emperors, and even Adolf Hitler, who believed it held the key to world domination. The spear's origins are shrouded in mystery, with some accounts claiming it was forged by Phineas, the son of Eleazar, while others state it was the spear used by the Roman centurion Longinus to pierce Christ's side. The spear is often associated with both immense power and terrible consequences, as those who possess it are believed to be destined for greatness but also face ultimate ruin. Its significance lies in its connection to the Passion of Christ and its perceived ability to influence the course of history. The spear is a potent symbol of faith, power, and the eternal struggle between good and evil. The blade is believed to contain a nail from the True Cross, further enhancing its sacred nature and perceived powers. Legends surrounding the spear continue to fascinate and inspire, solidifying its place as one of the most iconic and sought-after relics in Christian mythology and history.
subtitle: The spear that pierced Christ's side.
material: Iron, Steel, Possibly containing a nail from the True Cross
origin: Spear used to pierce Jesus Christ's side during crucifixion.
currentLocation: Unknown, various museums and private collections have claimed ownership.

keyMyths.title: The Piercing of Christ
keyMyths.description: Longinus, a Roman centurion, pierced Jesus' side with the spear during the crucifixion, confirming his death. This act is central to the spear's significance.

keyMyths.title: Hitler's Obsession
keyMyths.description: Adolf Hitler believed the Spear of Destiny held immense power and sought to possess it, believing it would grant him control over the world. He acquired a spear believed to be the Holy Lance, fueling the legend.

sources.author: Gospel of John
sources.description: Account of the crucifixion and the piercing of Jesus' side.
sources.text: Account of the crucifixion and the piercing of Jesus' side.

sources.author: Trevor Ravenscroft
sources.description: A popular book exploring the legends and history of the spear.
sources.text: A popular book exploring the legends and history of the spear.
